back_translation: Naomi had a relative on her husband's side, a man with wealth and status from the clan of Elimelech, named Boaz.

Ruth the Moabite woman said to Naomi, "Please let me go out to the field and gather the heads of grain that have fallen, following after someone who will show me kindness." Naomi replied, "Go on, my child."
So she went out and gathered heads of grain in the field behind the harvesters, and by chance she came to the portion of the field belonging to Boaz, who was from the clan of Elimelech.
Behold, Boaz came from Bethlehem and said to the harvesters, "May the LORD be with you all." They replied, "May the LORD bless you."
Boaz then asked his servant who was in charge of the harvesters, "To whom does this young woman belong?"
The foreman of the harvesters answered, "She is the Moabite young woman who returned with Naomi from the land of Moab.
She said, 'Please let me gather heads of grain and collect among the sheaves behind the harvesters.' She came in and has been here from morning until now, resting only briefly in the shelter."
Boaz then said to Ruth, "Listen, my child. Do not go gleaning in another's field, and do not go away from here. Stay close to my female servants.

Keep watching the field they are harvesting, and follow them. I have already commanded my young male servants not to touch you. When you are thirsty, go to the jars and drink the water my young male servants have drawn."
She then bowed her face down to the ground and said to him, "Why have I received such kindness in your sight that you regard me, even though I am a foreigner?"

Boaz answered her, "I have heard the whole story of all you have done for your mother-in-law since your husband died. You left your father, mother, and the land of your birth, and came to live with a people you had never known before.
May the LORD repay your deeds, and may your reward be full from the LORD, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come to take refuge."

She replied, "May I continue to receive kindness in your sight, my lord, for you have comforted me and spoken kindly to your female servant, even though I do not have status equal to one of your female servants."

When mealtime came, Boaz said to her, "Come here; take some bread and dip it in the vinegar." So she sat beside the harvesters, and he handed her roasted grain. She ate until satisfied and still had some left over.
When Ruth got up to glean again, Boaz instructed his young male servants, "Even if she gathers among the sheaves, do not rebuke her.
Moreover, deliberately pull out some grain stalks from the bundles for her, and leave them behind for her to gather. Do not rebuke her at all."
Ruth gathered heads of grain in the field until evening. When she beat out what she had gathered, it came to about an ephah of barley.
She carried the grain back to the city. Her mother-in-law saw what she had gathered, and she also brought out and gave to Naomi what was left over from her meal.

Her mother-in-law asked her, "Where did you gather grain today, and where did you work? Blessed be the man who took notice of you." So she told her mother-in-law with whom she had worked, and she said, "The name of the man with whom I worked today is Boaz."

Naomi said to her daughter-in-law, "May he receive blessing from the LORD, who has not abandoned His steadfast love toward both the living and the dead." Naomi continued, "This man is a close relative of ours; he is one of our kinsman-redeemers."
Ruth the Moabite woman said, "Moreover, he told me, 'Stay close to my young male servants until they have finished gathering all my harvest.'"
Naomi then said to Ruth her daughter-in-law, "My child, it is good that you have gone out with his female servants, so that no one will harm you in another's field."
So Ruth stayed close to the female servants of Boaz, gathering heads of grain until the barley harvest and the wheat harvest were finished. And she lived with her mother-in-law.
